reason: Obligation is structurally unnecessary under the current concept system because it collapses into duty as owed conduct, overlaps with responsibility as answerability, and is already handled by the runtime as authored ambiguity.
systemImpact: Reintroducing obligation as a first-class concept would blur the duty/responsibility boundary, duplicate existing rule-binding coverage, and destabilize the current authored ambiguity contract.
removalTestResult: The system retains rule binding, owed-conduct representation, and answerability handling without a standalone obligation concept. The current runtime already works by treating obligation as authored ambiguity between duty and responsibility.
